summary: Comprehensive research session on AI video generation tools as of March 2026
outcome: Documented all major proprietary and open-source tools with pricing, capabilities, quality rankings, limitations, and use cases. Key findings: Veo 3.1 leads on resolution (4K native), Kling 2.6 leads on video length (2 min), Seedance 2.0 (ByteDance) is the most notable early-2026 entrant, Wan 2.2 is the top open-source option. Audio generation is now standard across top tools. Main limitations: coherence beyond ~10s, creative control, compute cost, human motion artifacts.
follow_up: Deep dive into API integration patterns; benchmark testing across tools for specific use cases

summary: Researched Flux 2 (BFL) API, credit system, and programmatic usage for user with 2000-credit monthly plan
outcome: BFL API at api.bfl.ai/v1, auth via x-key header, 1 credit=/bin/zsh.01. FLUX.2 Pro ~/bin/zsh.055/image (~3600 imgs/month), FLUX.1.1 Pro ~/bin/zsh.04 (~5000 imgs/month), schnell /bin/zsh.015 (~13300/month). No official Python SDK — raw requests or fal-client work. fal.ai is 30-50% cheaper for same models. Official ComfyUI nodes: black-forest-labs/bfl-comfy-nodes. Prompt meta: no negative prompts, guidance scale 4.5 default, hex colors work, layer prompts subject>setting>lighting>style.
follow_up: Verify BFL API key location at dashboard.bfl.ai; test fal-client Python integration; consider building batch generation script
